channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Blockbuster ratings start to vindicate NBC’s $7.75 billion bet on the Olympics
item.description: NBC averaged 24 million viewers across its prime afternoon coverage and Primetime in Milan, a 94% improvement over the 2022 Beijing Games.

item.title: France Summons U.S. Ambassador Over Comments on Activist’s Killing
item.description: Charles Kushner, President Trump’s envoy to Paris, was called in after the State Department cited “violent radical leftism” in the beating death of Qu...

item.title: BAFTAs 2026: The Best and Worst Moments
item.description: Alan Cumming forced fishy British snacks onto movie stars. Paddington Bear presented an award. And a racist slur and swearing.

item.title: Pizzoccheri Was Prominent at Winter Olympics in Italy
item.description: Pizzoccheri, a hearty buckwheat pasta, graced restaurant menus and the athletes’ cafeterias. A chef hopes it can put a spotlight on the valley where i...

item.title: Self-driving taxis hit London, a city with such complex streets that it has a ‘Knowledge’ test that takes cabbies years to pass
item.description: Self-driving taxis are “a solution looking for a problem,” said Steven McNamara, general secretary of the Licensed Taxi Drivers' Association.

item.title: Mexico’s latest cartel violence prompts fears of ‘narcoterrorism’ in replay 1990s Colombia
item.description: The Mexican army killed the country’s most powerful cartel leader and one of the United States’ most wanted fugitives on Sunday, notching a major vict...

item.title: How ‘dumb money’ took over stock markets: $5.4 trillion of retail activity took place in 2025
item.description: All told, money coming into the market from individual investors jumped about 50% from 2023 to early 2025, JPMorgan found.

item.title: Northeast Corridor gathers snow at 3 inches an hour as National Weather Service calls traffic ‘nearly impossible’
item.description: Blizzard warnings stretched from Maryland to Maine, while over 5,000 flights were canceled.

item.title: The Human Cost of the Trump Administration’s War on Science
item.description: The ripple effects of cutting H.I.V. research funding.

item.title: Live Updates: Operation That Killed Cartel Boss Left Dozens Dead, Mexican Authorities Say
item.description: Officials said at least 62 people died in the raid that caught “El Mencho,” the longtime head of a powerful cartel, and the violence that followed.

item.title: Duterte Won’t Appear at Hearing as I.C.C. Weighs Evidence Against Him
item.description: The former leader of the Philippines faces a possible trial for the deaths of thousands during his war on drugs. Supporters and opponents gathered in ...

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Is the Most Patriotic Show on Television
item.description: An emergency room that’s a mirror.

item.title: France Summons U.S. Ambassador Over Comments on Activist’s Killing
item.description: Charles Kushner, President Trump’s envoy to Paris, was called in after the State Department cited “violent radical leftism” in the beating death of Qu...

item.title: Supreme Court to Weigh Oil-Industry Effort to End a Major Climate Suit
item.description: The case could have significant bearing on a range of other lawsuits brought against the fossil fuel industry by cities and states across the country.
